informal_statement.text: Given a slice of closed-open intervals [start, end), return the maximum number of mutually non-overlapping intervals selectable from the input.
preconditions: for every input interval, start < end
required_properties: the returned count equals the size of some subset of pairwise non-overlapping intervals from the input
no subset of pairwise non-overlapping intervals from the input is strictly larger than the returned count
intervals sharing only an endpoint (interval i ends where interval j starts) are considered non-overlapping
edge_cases: all intervals mutually disjoint (result equals len)
all intervals overlap a common point (result is 1)
intervals touching at a single endpoint

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: every interval satisfies start < stop.
Feasibility: the returned count is witnessed by some subset of the input that is pairwise non-overlapping.
common_failure_modes: confusing index selections with value selections

semantic_units.description: Optimality: no pairwise non-overlapping subset of the input is strictly larger than the returned count.
common_failure_modes: stating only feasibility (admits the trivial return 0)

semantic_units.description: Endpoint convention: two intervals that share only an endpoint are non-overlapping.
common_failure_modes: using strict inequality instead of non-strict at the boundary

semantic_units.description: Empty input: the result is 0 when the input is empty.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (ivs : Intervals), (∀ i ∈ ivs, i.start < i.stop) → ∃ (sel : List Nat), Feasible ivs sel ∧ sel.length = intervalScheduling ivs
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When every input interval has strictly positive length, there exists a feasible selection whose size equals the returned count.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (ivs : Intervals) (sel : List Nat), (∀ i ∈ ivs, i.start < i.stop) → Feasible ivs sel → sel.length ≤ intervalScheduling ivs
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When every input interval has strictly positive length, no feasible selection is strictly larger than the returned count.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (a b : Interval), a.stop = b.start → NonOverlap a b
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: Intervals touching at a single endpoint are non-overlapping.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem maxNonOverlapping_spec (intervals : List (Int × Int)) (start end : Int) : True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: State the overall goal: from a list of closed-open intervals [s,e) (represented as (s,e)), compute the maximum size of a subset of pairwise non-overlapping intervals.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: def nonOverlapping (I : List (Int × Int)) : Prop := ∀ ⦃a b⦄, a ∈ I → b ∈ I → a ≠ b → let sa := a.1; let ea := a.2; let sb := b.1; let eb := b.2; sa < ea ∧ sb < eb → (ea ≤ sb ∨ eb ≤ sa)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define what it means for a set/list of intervals to be mutually non-overlapping: for any two distinct intervals, one ends at or before the other begins (using closed-open semantics).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: def feasibleSubset (intervals : List (Int × Int)) (S : List (Int × Int)) : Prop := (∀ i, i ∈ S → i ∈ intervals) ∧ nonOverlapping S
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define feasibility: S is a subset of the input and its intervals are mutually non-overlapping.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: def optValue (intervals : List (Int × Int)) : Nat := Nat.findGreatest (by trivial)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the optimal value as the maximum cardinality among all feasible subsets (expressed as an optimization over Nat).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem precondition_closedOpen (intervals : List (Int × Int)) : (∀ i ∈ intervals, let s := i.1; let e := i.2; s < e) → True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume well-formed intervals: each interval is non-empty with start < end (so [start,end) is meaningful).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem impl_returns_nat (intervals : List (Int × Int)) : ∃ n : Nat, True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The Rust implementation returns a natural number representing the computed maximum count.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem impl_computes_greedy_opt (intervals : List (Int × Int)) (n : Nat) : True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Characterize the implementation’s computed value n as the result of the intended greedy algorithm (e.g., sorting by end time and repeatedly selecting the next interval with earliest finishing time compatible with the current end).

Prove the key greedy invariant: after processing a prefix of intervals (sorted by end), the algorithm’s chosen set is feasible and has maximal size among all feasible choices using that prefix with the same current end bound.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem output_is_feasible (intervals : List (Int × Int)) : let chosen := (/* intervals selected by the implementation */); feasibleSubset intervals chosen
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Show that the subset of intervals actually selected by the implementation is feasible (non-overlapping and contained in the input).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem output_cardinality (intervals : List (Int × Int)) : (/* implementation output */) = chosen.length
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Relate the numeric output to the length/cardinality of the selected set produced by the implementation.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem greedy_optimality (intervals : List (Int × Int)) : (∀ S, feasibleSubset intervals S → S.length ≤ (/* implementation output */))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove optimality: any feasible subset S has size at most the algorithm’s output.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem output_equals_optValue (intervals : List (Int × Int)) : (/* implementation output */) = optValue intervals
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Conclude that the implementation’s output equals the defined optimum value (maximum cardinality over all feasible subsets).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem empty_or_singleton (intervals : List (Int × Int)) : (intervals = [] ∨ intervals.length = 1) → (/* implementation output */) = intervals.length
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Verify correctness on base cases such as empty input and singleton input.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem boundary_nonOverlap_closedOpen (a b : Int × Int) : let sa := a.1; let ea := a.2; let sb := b.1; let eb := b.2; sa < ea → sb < eb → (ea = sb) → (ea ≤ sb ∨ eb ≤ sa)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Ensure the non-overlap relation uses closed-open semantics correctly: intervals that touch at a point (end == other start) are allowed to be selected together.
